summary.intent: Implement Task #286 — the framework-agnostic Table state machine for the ars-ui component library, including full state/event transitions, API attribute methods, spec drift fixes, and a complete test suite.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the Table agnostic core (~1843 LOC in mod.rs, ~1990 LOC in tests.rs), 126+ tests including snapshots/spec-conformance/proptest, fixed 11+ spec drifts, addressed 5 rounds of Codex review (26 total findings across 5 passes), and pushed PR #651 with all xci-fast gates passing. Round 5 fixes were implemented but the final commit/push/reply cycle was cut off at session end.

repo: Tabs (navigation/tabs/mod.rs) is the canonical structural template for new complex components — section ordering (State→Event→Messages→Props→Context→Part→Machine→Api), TransitionPlan::context_only() usage, SyncProps + SyncControlledValue pattern, and on_props_changed override all follow its layout

Bindable<T> controlled/uncontrolled contract: set() on a controlled Bindable is a no-op — get() returns the parent-controlled value, so any internal state derived from the Bindable (e.g. selection_state.selected_keys) must be re-synced from get() after every event that touches it, not just after set() calls

selection::Set is #[non_exhaustive] — match arms require a wildcard fallback; toggle() on selection::State requires a Collection generic the Table doesn't own, so Table calls is_selected + select/deselect directly

SelectAll semantics split by SelectAllMode: AllVisible materializes Set::Multiple(ctx.rows) on select-all; AllData preserves Set::All and individual deselects are rejected at the core layer (adapter handles exclusions via BulkSelection §5.2)

type Effect = NoEffect is correct when all transitions are context_only — adapters read ctx.focused_cell/focused_row/focused_col and apply DOM focus directly; widening to a typed enum is required only when ScrollToRow(Key) is added
Direction::Auto must be resolved to Direction::Ltr or Direction::Rtl in init() via env.locale — the Auto variant cannot persist in Context
cargo xtask spec validate checks frontmatter; cargo xtask spec compile-snippets checks code blocks in markdown specs — both must pass as part of xci-fast
proptest tests are marked #[ignore = "proptest — nightly extended-proptest job"] and excluded from the standard test run
cargo xci-fast is the 10-check pre-push gate; cargo fmt must run before it — the formatter catches style issues that block the gate but that clippy does not catch

CLAUDE.md mandates: post-implementation-audit before user review, snapshot-reviewed label on any PR creating .snap files, waiting-for-codex-review after first push; Codex review is triggered via @codex review comment and cycles through 👀 → 👍 or thread-drop
Codex thread reply convention: cite the commit SHA + the regression test name that locks in the fix; resolve via GitHub GraphQL API after all replies are posted

code.finding: Reference template for Table: provides the Machine impl pattern (init/transition/connect), SyncProps + SyncControlledValue events, on_props_changed override shape, and registered-keys pattern analogous to Table's SetRows

Part::ColumnHeader must carry a sortable: bool field — ConnectApi::part_attrs dispatcher must pass it through to column_header_attrs(); hardcoding sortable=true in the dispatcher silently breaks non-sortable column headers

is_row_selected / all_selected / some_selected must filter ctx.disabled_keys regardless of Set variant — Set::All would otherwise report disabled rows as selected, producing wrong aria-checked on row checkboxes
on_row_keydown and on_cell_keydown must skip disabled rows when computing next/prev/first/last via next_enabled_row / first_enabled_row helpers — raw index arithmetic lands on non-interactive rows
SyncProps must re-clamp all existing ctx.column_widths entries against the new min_column_width — changing the prop without re-clamping leaves stale narrow columns in the map
SetRows must clear focused_cell even when focused_row is None — Event::Focus can set focused_cell without setting focused_row, so the rebase check must be independent

SyncControlledSortDescriptor payload is Option<Option<SortDescriptor<String>>> — outer None exits controlled mode, inner None clears the sort value; the spec erroneously showed Option<SortDescriptor<String>>

1419-line spec had 11+ internal inconsistencies fixed in this PR: missing Context fields (dir, is_sorting, loading, column_widths, etc.), FocusCell hard-coding row 0, EscapeKey/FocusRow/FocusCell absent from §1.2 events table, build_sort_announcement referencing nonexistent locale/column_key field, misleading Scrolling state mention in §1.1.1

§3.4.2: non-sortable column headers MUST omit aria-sort entirely — the spec example was wrong and set aria-sort='none' unconditionally; test table_column_header_non_sortable_omits_aria_sort locks the correct behavior

workflow: TDD red-green enforced across all Codex rounds: write named failing tests first, verify compilation failure or assertion failure, then implement the fix — never implement without a failing test in place
cargo insta review (or --unreferenced=reject) must be run after snapshot-generating test changes; new fixtures require explicit acceptance before committing
Rebase onto main (not merge) is standard before any force-push; the spec_conformance.rs module registration is a common conflict point when parallel work adds sibling modules

Post-implementation-audit skill is a multi-phase structured process: Phase 1 (spec/impl drift table), Phase 2 iterative rounds (run until 2 consecutive clean rounds), Phase 3 (coverage measurement) — must complete all phases before presenting to user
Background polling scripts can be wiped between sessions (exit 127) — always verify the output file or run a direct gh CLI check before acting on a stored verdict
Codex thread reply format should cite: commit SHA + regression test name + one-line root-cause explanation — this speeds up the subsequent pass by giving Codex direct pointers to the fix

friction: Socket connection closed unexpectedly mid-session during initial plan drafting — the plan write was lost and had to be redone after the user explicitly asked 'Where is the plan?'
Background Codex polling scripts exited with code 127 twice (harness environment wiped between sessions), requiring manual script recreation and direct gh CLI re-verification each time
selection::State::toggle() requires a Collection generic that Table doesn't own — forced manual is_selected + select/deselect branches instead of a clean single-call delegate
Multiple clippy warning rounds accumulated across fix cycles: unnecessary *id derefs, unused mut, collapsible if-let — each blocked xci-fast and required an extra commit round-trip

5 full Codex review passes (26 total findings) before approaching 👍 — each pass required: fetch threads → write failing tests → implement → rebase → commit → force-push → reply inline → GraphQL resolve → re-trigger
Two mid-session rebases needed because concurrent PRs (#649, #652) landed on main while the Table branch was in the fix cycle
Branch coverage on table/mod.rs dropped to ~89.6% after Round 4 fixes, triggering the 90% crate-level gate — required targeted backfill tests to recover
open_items: ScrollToRow(Key) event and full Virtualizer integration are explicitly deferred — requires widening type Effect from NoEffect to a typed enum and pulling in ars-collections::Virtualizer plumbing; tagged for a follow-up issue

BulkSelection exclusion-set wrapper (§5.2) was intentionally excluded from the agnostic core — integration contract between Set::All and the adapter-side BulkSelection should be documented in a follow-up

row_link_attrs non-indexed variant delegates with row_index=0; row_link_attrs_indexed was added as the indexed variant but should become the primary API in a follow-up cleanup for virtualized table consistency

Round 5 Codex fixes (Part::ColumnHeader sortable field, disabled-key filtering in selection helpers, SyncProps min_column_width re-clamping, SetRows focused_cell rebase, spec event-table payload type update) were implemented and xci-fast passed, but the final commit/rebase/force-push/thread-reply/resolve cycle was cut off at session end — needs completion in the next session
